work_unit: runner-exec/scripts/exec.js ws error handler timer cleanup (SK-RE-010)
situation: audit-2026-07-02-003 finding SK-RE-010 (priority 8): exec.js:38 ws.on('error', reject) does not clearTimeout(timer) or ws.close(). If WebSocket errors after timeout timer starts, timer still fires → double-resolve on Promise + use-after-close on ws. Derived fix from deep audit of skills/runner-exec module.
action: Replaced bare `ws.on('error', reject)` with handler that clears timer (if set), safely closes ws (try/catch to avoid throw-on-already-closed), then rejects. Committed directly on existing worktree branch auto/opt-runner-exec-1782940040 (reused per route-fix rule — no public-interface touch). Pushed to origin per 2026-07-01 policy.
outcome_evidence: commit de4cb63 on auto/opt-runner-exec-1782940040 pushed to origin; git diff shows +5/-1 lines adding if(timer)clearTimeout + try{ws.close()}catch block before reject(err); main worktree restored clean via git checkout
